review_text: "Skip this place. I really wanted to like this resort, but so much was amiss. Reception on arrival was barely welcoming and told us little about the hotel. The room was lovely (we had a penthouse). It was impossible to book a restaurant on day 1. The next couple of days were booked up; they eventually found us one “due to a cancellation.” Yet half the dining room was empty, so something didn’t add up. We had a truly disturbing experience in the spa—one I’ve never encountered in my life. It’s not suitable to print the details. The hotel tried to make amends and the team member was asked to leave, but it left a bad taste over the rest of the holiday. I later discovered they only refunded £129 of the £258 they’d agreed to—disingenuous, given what happened. The restaurants (when you could get into them) were excellent. Yet other details were hit and miss—an announcement that children had to wear water diapers played on repeat all afternoon at one pool. When we left, the receptionist didn’t ask how our stay was, didn’t thank us, didn’t offer transport. Just three words: a flat, disinterested “OK that’s it.” These odd lapses—endless announcements, sullen reception, phantom bookings, and the spa issue—made the place feel more Jekyll than Hyde. For the first time after a holiday, I sat in the pickup car glad to be going home. With so many other resorts nearby, choose literally any of them instead."

review_text: "Amazing place that exceeds your expectations in every way. Almost everything was 5/5 in my view but what stands out most is the staff. They deserve a 5 /5. They truly go above and beyond to make your trip enjoyable. Super polite and accommodating. Hotel is super clean and very well looked after. Dining options? Imagine waking up in an episode of the Bear tv series. Endless options accommodating all sorts of diets with the best options. There are numerous bars and restaurants around the hotel with great offerings. Place is very family oriented. We were a family of 3; spouses and a 5-year old. My daughter had a blast. Water activity options are limited - 4 different types of big slides e.g. - but we didn't need more than that. My daughter was too young even for those 4 options. But, we enjoyed the kids club, playgrounds, nightly kids discos, numerous kids-friendly shows at the amphitheater etc. So, for a family like ours it was perfect. If you want to try jet ski or parachutes e.g. you can walk to the beach and leverage the neighboring hotel's offerings; very pricey though. 15 mins of jet ski costs you 100 Euros and a parachute ride (around 10 mins) costs the same; prices valid as of Aug 2024. The only thing we didn't enjoy (but I am not dropping a star off my review for it) was the drug store at the hotel. It basically is a super market that sells everything you need from toys, inflatables, sunscreen etc. But, everything is priced 3-4 times what you would pay in the UK."

review_text: "Nice hotel in a quite neighbourhood.The Nox Suite is a nice hotel in Antalya, near the shops. The room was spacious with cooking facilities (stove), a dishwasher washing machine and full size fridge-freezer. Plenty of storage space for clothes and in the kitchen area, space from groceries and cleaning supplies for the washing machine and dishwasher. An in-room safe was available. The bed was very comfortable. Housekeeping kept the room clean when requested. On site space for parking is available, although limited to a few spaces, that said, I still found a space. Parking is also possible the street outside. The air-conditioning worked well. Continental breakfast is available. Lastly, there is a pool with a patio area. The property is in a quiet neighbourhood. I recommend this property, the staff were friendly and helpful. The hotel is not far from Antalya Airport (AYT). Time to travel by car to and from the airport varies depending on the time of day, it took me about 25 minutes around 5.45am. On another occasion it took me longer due to traffic around the airport. review_text: "We just came back from a week in Antalya. We had an amazing time there. The hotel is beautiful, new and clean. Staff is really helpful and friendly and solved small snags quickly. We stayed in one of the 3-bedroom suites on the top floor and it was really comfortable to have such a good space. AC was powerful and showers had good pressure. The kitchen has some basic utensils. The only thing missing was a microwave oven. We used the washing machine and could dry clothes in the balcony. The pool is small but felt very private. My daughter improved her swimming in a week just by being able to spend time in the pool 2-3 hours a day. The breakfast was fine, I had omelettes every day made to order (had to pay extra but cheap). We rented a car and having an underground car park meant the car was never exposed to the sun. Lots of shops and restaurants nearby though and the TerraCity mall a short walk away. review_text: "This was my first time solo travelling, and I was sexually harassed, assaulted, and stalked by another guest during my stay at Holiday Inn Antalya Lara. Despite reporting this multiple times and even pointing out the individual, and despite the hotel confirming the incident through CCTV footage, the hotel refused to remove him and refused to call the police. They did not even offer to change my room, even after he discovered which floor I was staying on. Instead, staff were dismissive and kept making excuses like “we can’t do anything without management” while telling me “nothing will happen to you” and “this is a very safe hotel.” It gave the impression there is no policy to protect guests: even in cases of serious crime, you are left waiting for management to make a decision that may never come. The perpetrator remained in the hotel throughout my stay, approaching me again in front of staff, leaving me unsafe and unprotected. This not only put me in danger, but also other guests. After leaving, I contacted hotel management in writing but was ignored. I deliberately waited before posting this review to give them the chance to apologise, take responsibility and do the right thing. They did not. Guest safety is clearly not a priority here. I strongly advise against staying, especially for women travelling solo."

review_text: "Hotel is in a good location, 20 mins walk to the old town or jump a taxi right outside reception, plenty of small supermarkets, pharmacys and other shops close by for essentials. Its clean and looked after and being on my own, the area is safe at night, even at 3 in the morning!! They offer a free transfer to local beaches, didn't use it but its there if you want it. Room was good enough for the price, cleaned each day with fresh towels, coffee, tea, sugar and bottled water, buy some milk locally, aircon was good enough. The wifi was good, staff friendly, the pool although small was clean with comfy sun loungers. Breakfast was a little lacking, dont expect a full English, saying that you wont find one in resort either so its par for the course. Had a look at the evening meal and same goes so full board isnt worth it, there are loads of restaurants in the old town, didnt find them at first but when i did i had a ball, Dubh lin bar sells Guiness!! Roberts coffee is worth a visit for dinner, go to the end of the street towards the sea, cross the road and through the security barriers slightly to the right, modern and the foods good. Only downside is theres a Mineret very, very close to the hotel so 4.30am its full blast call to prayer, i was in direct line on the 6th floor overlooking the pool, you have been warned!! Happy travels."

review_text: "I’ll be honest, I chose this hotel because of the beautiful beach area. And it did not disappoint. The staff were incredible, from giving me great recommendations for dining (real Turkish Anatolian cuisine, not the tourist traps). And not one of those restaurants disappointed me. They went above and beyond with literally everything. It was super easy to get around. Just a short 5 minute walk through a beautiful park, which was great for sunset, into a charming Old Town. There are a few gems there for dining, but largely it’s great to explore the area. Taksi’s are inexpensive so it made it easy to explore. The staff was knowledgeable about the surrounding area and coordinated a private tour for me through an agency they use and I could not have been happier. Additionally, it was so hot, they referred me to Antalya Workshops where I went for a heat break in the afternoons and learned about Turkish coffee culture, made traditional Mosaic Lamps (great souvenir to take back) as well as Anatolian perfumes (which is also a thing in Turkey). All in all, the hotel was beautiful, service was excellent, staff was amazing. Breakfast at the hotel was lovely…other than that I ate out."

review_text: "Review Title: Disappointing Stay at Kiana Kaleici Hotel Our stay at Kiana Kaleici Hotel from August 2nd to 9th, 2024, was far from what we expected due to a series of issues that went unresolved. The mini-fridges in all three rooms didn’t work at all, leaving our drinks warm. It was frustrating to find that beverages left outside the fridge, cooled by the room’s air conditioning, were actually colder than those stored inside. The room listing promised free bottled water, yet after the first day, we were informed that we had to purchase our own water, which meant daily trips to a local store in 35-degree heat. The TV in one of our rooms was completely non-functional, and the other TVs only offered 3-4 local channels, causing us to miss the Olympics—despite the listing advertising 64-inch TVs with cable and premium channels. Instead, we found 24-inch TVs with basic channels. We also faced issues with the water pressure, which was inconsistent and often just trickled out, making it difficult to shower properly. Basic amenities like toothpaste, toothbrushes, and coffee were missing from the rooms. When we requested coffee the lady had to order it online. Only paper cups were available for hot drinks, we ad to request proper mugs. Additionally, the door to one of our rooms wouldn’t stay shut unless it was locked, which raised safety concerns. While the staff was polite, These unresolved issues made our stay uncomfortable and disappointing."

review_text: "The hotel is situated in a pretty street and is reminiscent of a bygone era. The photos don’t convey the feel of the property, the scent of the blossoms or the attentive staff, quietly going about their jobs and keen to assist when needed (helpful to use google translate sometimes!). Breakfast-a plentiful Turkish offering Rooms- simply furnished with air-con and we enjoyed one of the few with a balcony, which was much appreciated. Comfortable beds and black out curtains. Pool/gardens- a small pool but perfectly adequate for the guests using it. Stunning planting, trees and flowers, with an ample bar area with service to sunbeds! Restaurants- Tapas (ok), Pizza (very good but pasta only ok) and the fine dining Seresa ( excellent and worth the splurge) There are some decent restaurants in the area and a great many rug and souvenir shops!! review_text: "1st thing to state - after a long flight and arriving approaching midnight, the customer service of the staff was lacking. Prior to arriving, hotel had also been messaged asking for one of the sea facing rooms to have 2 single beds and they replied this would be done. Upon arrival, this request was declined as the sea view rooms don't have this option, to which I could only ask 'why did I receive this reply'. Instead of trying to solve this problem, I was told we'd be free to stay at another hotel so all in all - not the best of starts to my trip. Hotel room was nice - breakfast staff were nice - reception staff weren't as professional as could be. Toilet door & seat was loose but the room was nice and after my experience, I didn't think it necessary to say anything about this. The beach itself is a 2-3 minute walk and is pleasant. The area around Konyaalti is also pleasant but be mindful of the lift which has barely enough room for 2 person, let alone a family of four. If you don't mind being treated in a brusque manner by reception staff, then I'd recommend this hotel. Also be mindful that there is no safe in the hotel rooms. The hotel is clean and pleasant but having stayed at other hotels, I'll be looking elsewhere next time."

review_text: "Fantastic hotel, not in a prime location but if you are looking for an idyllic spot near the beach, dotted with restaurants and well equipped then you cant go wrong. Theres a taxi stop barely 250m away so if you need a ride just walk to the location and voila you can get wherever you need to. Theres a distinct smell of wet wood coming from the sewage which some may find horrible. Spa facilities are truly fantastic and the hotel all in all is economical, well managed and discreet. The cleaning staff would dilligently do their work when I was gone that not once did I have the chance to get acquainted. The tvs are not any good and have a propensity to release bombastic extraneous sounds. Balconies are spacious and so are the rooms. You will not be cramped for space. Three door cavernous wardrobes are installed so your stuff will never be just lying around unless you want it to be. All front facing staff speak adequate English, not sure what reviewers were complaining about. Yes staff at the spa don't for the most part but you can make do with linguistic barriers as Turkish terms are pretty much the same. Food at the ala carte restaurant is all right but contrary to claims made all personnel speak English."

review_text: "I want to begin by saying that the hotel staff were truly wonderful. Hasan, Omer, Serve, and another receptionist whose name I regretfully forgot, were all incredibly kind and helpful. review_text: "This was a family trip, and it’s actually my second time staying at this hotel – the first was 7 years ago. Right across the hotel there’s a shopping mall, games, and even a water park, which made it very convenient. The service is excellent, breakfast is delicious, and the staff are truly friendly and always willing to help. The food in the hotel is also very nice, and the prices are very reasonable. review_text: "My family and I enjoyed our four night stay at the Prime Hotel.The welcome at reception was professional and very friendly. The rooms are large and have a beautiful bathroom with shower that has excellent water pressure with beautiful sea views and the beds are super comfortable. The rooms are cleaned daily by friendly staff. My teenage children enjoyed the gym and used it most days which was open 24 hours. The pool area is private and very nice the pool is kept clean and clean towels was always available. The staff at reception always helped with recommendations for beaches , directions , transfers shuttles and tourist activities. There is a beautiful beach deck across the road from the hotel which is well worth it, and we were lucky to get some passes one day from reception. Several men were with one woman, and we could hear distressing screams late at night. When I called the reception, security removed the woman but took no action against the group’s behavior. Staff admitted they had been disturbing other guests all week, yet justified inaction because “they are guests.” As a woman, I expressed that I felt unsafe, but the only response was that the men would be leaving soon. review_text: "Perfect location to discover Antalya's heritage site. The hotel itself has an incredible story with a rich architecture from 3 different civilizations, a cute patio that the host will take time to tell you about. There are great restaurants, pubs, café (SIDD, a gem!), hookah places just around the corner. However for families with under age kids (ours are 3 and 1), it's not friendly. The room we were in (1st room by the main entrance) had poor isolation from outside, the floors cracked a lot, we could hear anyone speaking in the lobby, people entering and exiting the hotel, kids in the upper apartment laughing. There were 2 staircases in our apartment (one leading to the bedroom, the other one to the bathroom (3 stairs), it was a pure nightmare with our 1 year baby climbing staircases up and down. The upper level is a mezzanine with bars wide enough for a baby to go through. I was very stressed out inside the room but spent a bit of time outside in the patio, super chill! The other star was removed because of the lights coming through the entrance door. Baby woke up at 6am like it was 8am. As a couple with no kids, i would definitely stay there to be at the heart of the city. As a mom of 2 small kids, I'd book ourselves into a "proper" hotel room for another 4 years. Arda and the rest of the staff are super nice, I really dont wish to bring their rate down so I'm only removing 1 star."

Our check-in was smooth and the staff was helpful and kind. After checking into our room, we explored the property a little bit and enjoyed a dip in the pool and played some ping-pong. We ordered some food and decided to go ahead and eat in the room. We ordered a combination of room service, which is a little expensive but not too much and ordering some food in from outside. Then we decided to use the jacuzzi tub in the room. Unfortunately after about half an hour of use, while draining it, all the water went onto the floor instead of down the pipe resulting in our room flooding! Once again they were very accommodating and more worried about our items being damaged they were about the room being damaged, which was thoughtful. They took quick action and started vacuuming up the water and getting us a new accommodation. . This does lead, however, to my only real criticism of this hotel. Everything looks very glamorous on the outside, but has major design flaws and lack of follow through with execution of construction. Full kitchen but not one piece of dishware or silverware. There’s not one pot or pan to cook on a full stove. Final thoughts include the shower is very nice, the beds comfortable and the room extremely spacious for Turkey. Also in the morning breakfast was delicious."
